title: STANDARD FOR CANNED CHESTNUTS AND CANNED CHESTNUT PUREE CXS 145-1985

sections.section: STANDARD FOR CANNED CHESTNUTS AND CANNED CHESTNUT PUREE CXS 145-1985
sections.summary: Title: 'Standard for Canned Chestnuts and Canned Chestnut Puree (CXS 145-1985)', adopted 1985 and amended in 2015, 2017 and 2022.

2022 amendment notice: following decisions at the Forty‑fifth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission (Dec 2022), amendments were made to Section 7.2 concerning labelling of non‑retail containers.

Defines canned chestnuts (from Castanea crenata or Castanea sativa, shelled, pellicled or unpellicled, packed with/without water/sugars/seasonings and heat‑processed) and canned chestnut puree (mechanically pureed pulp packed with/without sugars and heat‑processed); specifies styles for chestnuts (Whole, Broken) and puree (Sweetened ≥12 °Brix, Unsweetened ≥10 °Brix) and permits other distinct presentations provided they meet the standard and are adequately described on the label.

Product definition: canned chestnuts are prepared from fresh, sound, mature Castanea crenata or Castanea sativa chestnuts, shelled and possibly pellicled, packed with or without water/sugars/seasonings and heat‑processed to prevent spoilage; canned chestnut puree is the sieved or mechanically pulped form of that product, similarly packed and heat‑processed.

Defines permitted styles for canned chestnuts and purees: 'Whole' and 'Broken' for chestnuts; 'Sweetened' (≥12°Brix) and 'Unsweetened' (≥10°Brix) for puree; and allows other distinctive presentations if they meet the standard and are clearly labelled.
Permits canned chestnuts to be packed as 'Whole' (pellicled or unpellicled, may be trimmed) or 'Broken' (small pieces that may be non‑uniform in size/shape).

Specifies canned chestnut puree styles: 'Sweetened' (contains sugars listed in Section 2.1(b) and must have not less than 12% total soluble solids/12°Brix) and 'Unsweetened' (no added sugars and must have not less than 10% total soluble solids/10°Brix).

Allows other presentations of the product provided they are sufficiently distinctive from prescribed forms, comply with all other standard requirements, and are adequately described on the label to avoid misleading consumers.

Sets composition and quality requirements: packing media may be water or water with specified nutritive sweeteners and are classified by Brix; puree sugars limited to 2% and salt to 1%; and specifies organoleptic, texture and size uniformity criteria plus defect limits (e.g., ≤14% mass defects, ≤20% non‑whole for 'whole' style), classification of 'defectives' and lot acceptance by sampling with an AQL of 6.5.

Permits packing medium to be either water alone or water with one or more specified nutritive sweeteners (sucrose, invert sugar syrup, dextrose, dried glucose syrup, glucose syrup, fructose, fructose syrup, honey).

sections.section: 2.2 Classification of packing media when nutritive sweeteners are added
sections.summary: When nutritive sweeteners are added, requires classification of liquid packing media by cut‑out strength (Brix) into categories (slightly sweetened water, light syrup, heavy syrup, extra heavy syrup) and states cut‑out strength is determined on average with no container allowed to have a Brix value below the next lower category.

sections.section: 2.2.1 When nutritive sweeteners are added to water, the liquid media shall be classified on the basis of the cut-out strength as follows
sections.summary: Specifies Brix ranges for packing media categories when sweeteners are added: slightly sweetened water (not less than 10°Brix but less than 14°Brix), light syrup (not less than 14°Brix but less than 18°Brix) and heavy syrup (not less than 18°Brix but less than 22°Brix).

Limits other ingredients: canned chestnut puree may contain the sugars listed in Section 2.1(b) up to 2% of total net contents, and canned chestnuts and puree may contain salt (sodium chloride) not exceeding 1% of total net contents.

Establishes quality criteria covering colour, flavour, texture and size uniformity and sets defect limits (including ≤14% mass defects and ≤20% non‑whole for 'whole' style), defines a 'defective' container and requires lot acceptance by sampling using an AQL of 6.5.
Requires that, when no colour is added, canned chestnuts and puree have the normal colour characteristic of the varieties used and that browning or discolouration are regarded as defects.
Requires canned chestnuts and chestnut puree to have a normal flavour and odour free from flavours and odours foreign to the products.

Requires canned chestnuts to have a reasonably uniform, fairly thick texture that is neither excessively firm nor unreasonably soft, and requires chestnut puree to have uniform consistency and particle size.

sections.section: ## 2.4.3.2 Canned chestnut puree shall have a uniform consistency and particle size.
sections.summary: States that canned chestnut puree shall have a uniform consistency and particle size.
For 'Whole' style, requires that in 95% by count of the most uniform units the weight of the largest unit shall be no more than twice the weight of the smallest unit.

Requires the product to be substantially free from defects (harmless plant material, shell, pellicle, blemishes, splits, discolouration), treats slight syneresis in puree as acceptable, and limits certain defects to not more than 14% by mass on the net drained weight and not more than 20% non‑whole chestnuts by net drained weight for the 'whole' style.

Defines a 'defective' as any container failing to meet one or more applicable quality requirements in Sections 2.4.1–2.4.5, except that extraneous plant material is assessed on the average of the entire sample.

Specifies lot acceptance: a lot meets the quality requirements when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan using an AQL of 6.5, with reference to Codex methods of analysis and sampling.

Lists permitted food additives and maximum levels: sodium polyphosphate (chelating agent) limited by GMP; antioxidants including L‑ascorbic acid (300 mg/kg expressed as ascorbic acid) and sodium ascorbate (alone or combined); and acidifying agents such as citric acid and malic acid (malic limited by GMP).
Permits sulphur dioxide as a bleaching agent at up to 30 mg/kg expressed as SO2 but explicitly states sulphur dioxide is not authorized for use in chestnut puree.

Firming agents listed in Table 1 and Table 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 04.2.2.4, or those listed in Table 3 of that General Standard, are permitted for use in foods conforming to this standard.
Maximum contaminant limits are specified as Lead (Pb) 1 mg/kg and Tin (Sn) 250 mg/kg.

Products must be prepared and handled per the General Principles of Food Hygiene and relevant Codex codes of practice, be free from objectionable matter to the extent possible under GMP, and when tested must not contain microorganisms, parasites, or microbial-derived substances in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.

Containers must be filled to specified minimums (see Section 6.1: product including packing medium not less than 90% of water capacity), lots are judged by an AQL-based sampling plan (AQL 6.5), and minimum drained-weight percentages apply (see Section 6.1.4: 60% for containers ≥300 ml water capacity, 55% for <300 ml) with compliance determined by average drained weight and absence of unreasonable shortages.

The container must be well filled with chestnuts or chestnut puree so that the product (including packing medium) occupies at least 90% of the container's water capacity, defined as the volume of distilled water at 20°C the sealed container will hold when completely filled.
Minimum fill requirement: product (including packing medium) must occupy not less than 90% of the container's water capacity (volume of distilled water at 20°C the sealed container will hold).
Any container that fails to meet the 90% minimum fill requirement specified in Section 6.1.1 is classified as a 'defective'.

A lot meets the minimum-fill requirement when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan using an AQL of 6.5, per relevant Codex methods of analysis and sampling.

Minimum drained-weight requirements: for containers with water capacity ≥300 ml the drained weight shall be at least 60% of water capacity, and for <300 ml at least 55%; compliance is established when the average drained weight of examined containers meets the minimum and there is no unreasonable shortage in individual containers.

In addition to the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods, this standard requires specific labelling for chestnuts/chestnut puree including mandated product names, declaration of style and terms like 'unpellicled' where appropriate, clear declaration of packing medium using prescribed terms, and non-retail container labelling in accordance with the General Standard for Labelling of Non-Retail Containers (CXS 346-2021).

The product name must be 'chestnuts' or 'chestnut puree' as defined in Sections 1.1.1/1.1.2, and the style and any additional words needed to avoid consumer confusion must be declared in close proximity to the name.

sections.section: 7.1.1 The name of the product shall be 'chestnuts' if it meets the definition in Section 1.1.1 or 'chestnut puree' if it meets the definition in Section 1.1.2.
sections.summary: Product naming requirement: label must state 'chestnuts' or 'chestnut puree' according to the applicable product definition.

sections.section: 7.1.2 The style, as appropriate, shall be declared as a part of the name or in close proximity to the name:
sections.summary: The product style (e.g., 'Whole', 'Brokens', 'Sweetened', 'Unsweetened') must be declared as part of or close to the name, and additional wording should be added where necessary to avoid misleading or confusing the consumer.

sections.section: 7.1.3 The term 'unpellicled' shall be declared as appropriate as part of the name or in close proximity to the name.
sections.summary: The term 'unpellicled' must be declared as part of, or in close proximity to, the product name when appropriate.

sections.section: 7.1.4 The packing medium shall be declared as part of the name or in close proximity to the name:
sections.summary: The packing medium must be declared as part of or close to the product name, with specific wording required depending on the medium and sweetening.

sections.section: 7.1.4.1 When the packing medium is composed of water, the packing medium shall be declared as:
sections.summary: When the packing medium is water, it must be declared as 'In water' or 'Packed in water'.

sections.section: 7.1.4.2 When nutritive sweeteners are added to water, the packing medium shall be declared as:
sections.summary: When nutritive sweeteners are added to the packing water, the packing medium must be declared using terms such as 'Slightly sweetened water', 'Water slightly sweetened', 'Extra light syrup', 'Light syrup', 'Heavy syrup' or 'Extra heavy syrup'.

sections.section: 7.1.4.3 When the packing medium contains no added sweetening agents, the term 'no added sugar' or other words of similar import may be used in association with, or in close proximity to the name of the food.
sections.summary: If no nutritive sweetening agents are added to the packing medium, the claim 'no added sugar' or similar wording may be used in association with or close to the product name.
Labelling of non-retail containers should comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Compliance with this standard must be checked using the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard.
